JP MORGAN CHASE BANK N.A.'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED IS GRANTED. THE TRUTH OF ANY MATTERS SPECIFIED IN THE REQUESTS IS DEEMED ADMITTED IN LIGHT OF RESPONDING PARTY'S FAILURE TO RESPOND TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS BEFORE THE DATE OF HEARING. SEE CCP 2033.280(B). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to Defendant Frida N. Gilchrist

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: D. Carter;

Matter was resolved on the pleadings. Court did not go on the record.

Ruonan Wang, Esq. appeared on behalf of Plaintiff JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A.
No appearance by Defendant Frida N. Gilchrist.

Plaintiff JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. exhibits for the Court's review:

The matter is submitted.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff JPMorgan chase Bank, N.A. and against Defendant Frida N. Gilchrist.

Plaintiff JPMorgan chase Bank N.A. is awarded judgment against Defendant Frida N. Gilchrist as follows:

Damages/Principal: $1,489.33
Pre-Judgment Interest: $0.00
Costs: $0.00
Total Judgment: $1,489.33

Proposed Judgment was submitted and signed by the Court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted;
Off Calendar Feb-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'s Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

JP Morgan Chase Bank N.A.'s unopposed motion to deem requests for admissions admitted is granted. The truth of any matters specified in the requests is deemed admitted in light of responding party's failure to respond to the requests for admissions before the date of hearing. See CCP 2033.280(b).

CCP 2033.280(c) states that a court order for sanctions is mandatory in this circumstance. The court orders defendant Frida N. Gilchrist to pay $50 to JP Morgan Chase, by payment to its counsel, within 20 court days of the date of notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-31-2024 Set For Trial On Apr-01-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
